title: Project Initiation and Planning
description:
The demand for skilled project managers is surging, with the global economy projected to require an additional 25 million professionals in this field by 2030 (Project Management Institute). This course provides the ideal introduction to project management initiation and planning - a key stage in the success of a project. It is tailored for beginners, professionals just starting their careers, or individuals looking to switch paths—no prior experience is required.  
 In this course, you’ll explore and compare predictive and adaptive project management models. You’ll discover how to avoid common causes of project failure, lead effective meetings, and motivate your team to ensure project success.  
 You’ll look at the documentation and learn how to create the essential documents required during the project initiation phase, including the business case, project charter, project brief, stakeholder register, and engagement plan. You’ll also prepare supporting documents for developing project scope, schedules, and budgets.  
 As you learn, you’ll apply your new knowledge in hands-on labs to gain practical experience in real-world scenarios. Plus, you’ll complete a final project, so you have a portfolio of widely used project initiation and planning assets that demonstrate your skills to potential employers.  
 If you’re looking to get your first job in project management and are keen to build specific project initiation and management skills, ENROLL TODAY and add skills to your resume that employers are looking for in less than 6 weeks!
